# <!--name-->github/notify-pr-is-fork<!--/name-->

[![usages](https://img.shields.io/badge/usages-white?logo=githubactions&logoColor=blue)](https://github.com/search?q=elastic%2Foblt-actions%2Fgithub%2Fnotify-pr-is-fork+%28path%3A.github%2Fworkflows+OR+path%3A**%2Faction.yml+OR+path%3A**%2Faction.yaml%29&type=code)
[![test-github-notify-pr-is-fork](https://github.com/elastic/oblt-actions/actions/workflows/test-github-notify-pr-is-fork.yml/badge.svg?branch=main)](https://github.com/elastic/oblt-actions/actions/workflows/test-github-notify-pr-is-fork.yml)

<!--description-->
Notify that the pull request is from a fork and that e2e tests require a feature branch in the main repository.
<!--/description-->

## Inputs

<!--inputs-->
| Name           | Description              | Required | Default               |
|----------------|--------------------------|----------|-----------------------|
| `github-token` | The GitHub access token. | `false`  | `${{ github.token }}` |
<!--/inputs-->

## Outputs

<!--outputs-->
| Name | Description |
|------|-------------|
<!--/outputs-->

## Behavior

- Emits a workflow warning that e2e tests require a feature branch in the main repository.
- Adds a GitHub comment explaining that forks are not supported for running the e2e tests.
- The comment is added only once per pull request (subsequent runs skip it if the comment already exists).

## Usage

<!--usage action="elastic/oblt-actions/**" version="env:VERSION"-->
```yaml
---
name: notify-fork

on:
  pull_request:
    types:
      - opened
      - synchronize
      - reopened

permissions:
  contents: read

jobs:
  notify:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: github.event.pull_request.head.repo.fork == true
    permissions:
      pull-requests: write
    steps:
      - uses: elastic/oblt-actions/github/notify-pr-is-fork@v1
```
<!--/usage-->
